label: Ukraine GDP Change 2022
change: Largest single-year drop in modern Ukrainian history

label: Ukraine GDP Growth 2023
change: Partial recovery driven by defense industry and western aid

label: Ukraine Reconstruction Cost Estimate
change: Needed over 10 years; equivalent to 3× pre-war Ukrainian GDP

label: Frozen Russian State Assets
change: Primarily €210B at Euroclear Belgium; generating ~$3B/year interest

label: US Military Aid to Ukraine (total)
change: Includes Biden-era packages + $61B April 2024 package; minerals deal (Apr 2025) counts future US military aid as fund contributions

label: EU Total Aid to Ukraine (all types)
change: EU surpassed US as largest single aid provider in 2024; includes €50B 4-year facility. NATO targeting additional $15B for Ukraine in 2026.
source: Kiel Institute for the World Economy (Feb 2026)

label: Russia Defense Spending (% of GDP)
change: Up from 3.6% pre-war; 41% of all federal budget on military/security

label: Russian Central Bank Interest Rate
change: Cut from 21% peak (7th cut since Jun 2025); Bank of Russia easing cycle — 15.5% Feb 2026, then 15% Mar 20, 2026 as economy slows and inflation moderates to ~5.9%. Oil export disruptions from Ukrainian drone campaign compound economic pressure.

label: Ukraine War Cost per Day (Russia)
change: Estimated daily Russian war expenditure; driving budget deficit and depleting National Wealth Fund

label: Russian National Wealth Fund
change: Kremlin acknowledged fund running dry; at record withdrawal pace in early 2026. ~40% of Russian oil export capacity halted (Mar 25) from Ukrainian drone strikes on Ust-Luga and Primorsk ports plus tanker seizures — described as most severe disruption in modern Russian history.

label: Ukrainians in Poverty
change: Up from 5.5% pre-war; 7.1M pushed into poverty

label: G7 Loan to Ukraine (backed by frozen assets)
change: Finalized Oct 2024; US contributed $20B; secured by Russian asset interest (~$3B/year)

label: Brent Crude Oil Price
change: Surged above $100 for first time since 2022 invasion; driven by Iran-US conflict closing Strait of Hormuz. US Treasury issued 30-day waiver on Russian seaborne crude Mar 13, 2026.

label: US–Ukraine Critical Minerals Investment Fund
change: Joint fund for Ukraine reconstruction backed by future mineral revenues. Future US military aid counts as US contribution. Ukraine not required to repay past aid. Minerals wealth estimated at $14.8T total.

label: Russia Gasoline Export Ban (April 2026)
change: Russia suspended all gasoline exports effective April 1, 2026 — direct consequence of Ukrainian drone strikes cutting domestic refining capacity and port export throughput
source: Russian Deputy PM Novak / Reuters / Bloomberg (Mar 30, 2026)
